symptoms.symptom_name: Non-traumatic bilateral bone pain with refusal to weight-bear

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Diffuse symmetrical bone pain from marrow expansion/periosteal infiltration is a classic paediatric ALL presentation.
Focal bone infection is usually unilateral and localised, not bilateral and diffuse.

symptoms.symptom_name: New non-blanching petechial rash with spontaneous bruising

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Petechiae and spontaneous bruising reflect severe thrombocytopenia from marrow failure.
Fever plus a non-blanching rash is a hallmark warning sign of invasive meningococcal/bacterial disease.
Petechiae fit ITP but the accompanying systemic features do not.
Fever in marrow failure reflects neutropenia and/or the malignant process itself.
Fever with a non-blanching rash mandates treating sepsis as a live emergency.
Symptomatic anaemia from marrow failure produces pallor, lethargy and tachycardia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of trauma to account for pain or bruising

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Spontaneous (non-traumatic) bruising and bone pain raise concern for a pathological rather than mechanical cause.
No trauma reported and the distribution/systemic features fit a haematological cause.
Poor response to simple analgesia is non-specific but underlines the severity and systemic nature of the illness.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hepatosplenomegaly on palpation (2 cm below costal margins)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Leukaemic infiltration of liver and spleen — a sealed clinician exam finding not obtainable via telehealth.
Generalised lymphadenopathy from lymphoid infiltration — sealed clinician exam finding.
Compensatory tachycardia of anaemia/systemic illness — clinician-measured vital, sealed; the AI infers distress from pallor and lethargy, not a measured heart rate.

symptoms.symptom_name: Full blood count and peripheral blood film (>20% circulating lymphoblasts)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Full blood count with peripheral blood film
investigation_details.expected_result: Cytopenias with circulating lymphoblasts; marrow shows >20% lymphoblasts on aspirate
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The defining diagnostic step is missed; cannot risk-stratify bleeding/sepsis without it.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Circulating and marrow lymphoblasts define acute lymphoblastic leukaemia — hospital investigation, never obtainable via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Bone pain + petechiae/bruising + fever + pallor/fatigue together is the classic paediatric acute-leukaemia presentation and mandates emergency assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count with peripheral blood film
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Defines the cytopenias and identifies circulating blasts; must be done tonight in an emergency setting — cannot be performed via telehealth.
Assesses bleeding risk alongside severe thrombocytopenia.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bone-marrow aspirate and trephine with immunophenotyping/cytogenetics
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Specialist-performed confirmatory test (>20% lymphoblasts); arranged by the paediatric haematology/oncology team, never via telehealth.
Fever with a non-blanching rash and probable neutropenia requires parallel sepsis evaluation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: At-home self-directed blood testing or imaging arranged remotely
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Nothing about this emergency should be routed to outpatient or self-arranged testing; it requires same-night emergency assessment.
